Entries.Name: Advanced Startup Desktop Shortcut
Entries.Description: Desktop shortcut that reboots into Advanced Startup
Entries.Detail: Creates a desktop shortcut that immediately reboots the PC into the Advanced Startup Options (Windows Recovery Environment) menu.
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Creates a shortcut that triggers an immediate reboot into the Windows Recovery Environment advanced startup menu. Useful when the F8 boot key is unavailable.

Entries.Name: Silent App Installing
Entries.Detail: Windows can silently install suggested apps in the background without asking you. Disabling stops this behavior.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Automatic silent install of suggested apps is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Stops Windows from installing suggested apps in the background without prompting. Prevents sponsored apps from appearing after a clean install or reset.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Microsoft consumer experiences are a Windows client feature.

Entries.Name: Active Hours
Entries.Detail: Defines the time range when Windows will not automatically restart your PC to finish installing updates.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Active hours adjusted automatically
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Tells Windows not to initiate automatic restarts during your specified hours. Updates will still install; only the forced-restart window is restricted.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Active hours are a Windows client update feature.

Entries.Name: Admin Approval Mode (UAC)
DisplayOptions: Notify when apps try to make changes
Notify when apps try to make changes (no dim)
Entries.Detail: Controls how User Account Control behaves when an app requests elevated privileges. Available levels include prompting for credentials, always notifying, the Windows default consent prompt, the same consent prompt without dimming the desktop, and never notifying.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: UAC notifies only when apps make changes on the secure desktop (Default)
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets how UAC handles elevation requests. Higher levels prompt with a credential dialog; lower levels use a consent prompt or bypass the prompt entirely. Disabling means any process can silently gain administrator rights.

Entries.Name: AutoPlay
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows automatically opens a prompt or launches an app when you insert a USB drive or other removable media.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: AutoPlay is enabled for all media and devices
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether Windows shows a dialog or launches an app automatically when removable media is inserted. Disable to always require a manual action.

Entries.Name: BSoD Stop Error Code
Entries.Description: Display stop error code on BSoD
Entries.Detail: Shows the specific error code on the blue screen of death instead of just the generic sad face emoji.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Stop error code not displayed on BSoD
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Adds the specific stop code to the blue screen display. Without it you only see a generic error page and must dig through event logs to find the code.

Entries.Name: Caps Lock
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the Caps Lock key is enabled. Disabling remaps the Caps Lock key so pressing it does nothing.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Caps Lock key is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Remaps Caps Lock to a no-op at the registry level. The key is still physically present but pressing it has no effect.

Entries.Name: Client for Microsoft Networks
Entries.Detail: The Microsoft Networks client that allows this PC to access files and printers shared by other Windows computers on the network.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Client for Microsoft Networks is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The SMB client component that allows this PC to connect to remote Windows file and printer shares. Disabling prevents all SMB outbound connections.

Entries.Name: Current Network Profile
Entries.Detail: Sets the network profile for your currently active connection. Private enables network discovery and sharing, Public restricts it.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Current network profile set to Public
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Private profile enables network discovery and file sharing. Public profile restricts both. This controls the active connection only, not future connections.

Entries.Name: Default Input Method
Entries.Detail: Sets the default keyboard input method Windows uses. English forces the English layout regardless of other installed languages.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Default input method: use language list
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Pins the keyboard input method so Windows uses the English layout by default even when other language packs are installed.

Entries.Name: Default Terminal App
Entries.Detail: Sets which terminal application opens when you launch Command Prompt or PowerShell from within Windows.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows Console Host is the default terminal app
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Selects which terminal host opens when Command Prompt or PowerShell is launched from within Windows, such as from the Run dialog or context menus.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Default terminal application policy is supported on Windows 10/11 client editions.

Entries.Name: Delivery Optimization
Entries.Detail: Windows can use your PC to share Windows Update download data with other PCs on your network or the internet. Disabling keeps your downloads private.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Delivery Optimization is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows this PC to serve Windows Update download data to other PCs on your LAN or the internet. Disabling keeps update bandwidth usage private to this machine.

Entries.Name: DNS Provider Presets
Entries.Detail: Sets DNS servers on every network adapter that is currently Up. Default leaves the current DNS settings unchanged, DHCP restores automatic DNS assignment, and the remaining choices apply common public resolver presets.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: DNS servers are obtained automatically
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Writes DNS server addresses to all network adapters that are currently up. Choose DHCP to revert to automatic assignment or Default to leave existing settings unchanged.

Entries.Name: F1 Help Lookup
Entries.Detail: Pressing F1 in File Explorer or on the desktop opens the Windows help website in your browser. Disabling prevents this browser launch.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: F1 help lookup is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Pressing F1 in File Explorer launches the Windows help website in the default browser. Disabling prevents this key from triggering browser launches.

Entries.Name: File and Printer Sharing (SMB Server)
Entries.Detail: Enables the SMB file server component that lets other computers on the network access shared folders and printers on this PC.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File and printer sharing (SMB) is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Enables the inbound SMB server so other machines on the network can access shares and printers on this PC. Disable if this machine does not need to serve files.

Entries.Name: Get Latest Updates ASAP
Entries.Description: Get latest updates as soon as available
Entries.Detail: Enrolls this PC to receive Windows updates as soon as they are released rather than waiting for a gradual rollout.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Latest updates not received as soon as available
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Enrolls the PC in the earliest-available update wave. Useful for staying current on security patches; carries higher risk of receiving patches before widespread testing has surfaced regressions.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Windows Update rollout preference is a Windows client setting.

Entries.Name: Download Updates Over Metered Connection
Entries.Description: Windows Update downloads over metered connections
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows Update downloads updates automatically when the network is metered. Disabling keeps update traffic off metered connections.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows Update does not download updates automatically over metered connections
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls automatic update downloads when on a metered connection. Disabling prevents update traffic from counting against a data cap.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Windows Update metered-connection behavior is a Windows client setting.

Entries.Name: Feature Update Deferral
Entries.Detail: Delays feature updates by 365 days. Disable to return to normal Windows update rollout behavior.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Feature updates are not deferred
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Delays major Windows version upgrades by up to 365 days. Useful for stability, but extended deferral can push the machine past a version's end-of-support date.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Feature update deferral is a Windows client Windows Update policy.

Entries.Name: Quality Update Deferral Period
Entries.Detail: Delays monthly quality updates by 4 or 7 days.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Quality updates are not deferred
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Delays monthly security and quality patches by 4 or 7 days. Gives time for early adopters to surface patch regressions before you apply.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Quality update deferral is a Windows client Windows Update policy.

Entries.Name: Hibernation
Entries.Detail: Saves the entire contents of RAM to disk and powers the PC off completely. The hibernation file can use several gigabytes of disk space.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Hibernation is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Full hibernation writes all RAM to hiberfil.sys and cuts power completely, unlike Fast Startup which only saves kernel state. The hibernation file can be several gigabytes depending on installed RAM.

Entries.Name: Hybrid Sleep
Entries.Detail: Combines Sleep and Hibernation by writing the session to disk while keeping the system in low-power S3. On wake the system resumes from RAM if power was retained, or from hiberfil.sys if power was lost. Mirrors the tracked Hybrid Sleep settings (powercfg subgroup SUB_SLEEP / setting HYBRIDSLEEP). Some hardware does not support Hybrid Sleep and the toggle becomes a no-op there.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Hybrid Sleep on (desktop default)
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Disabling Hybrid Sleep lets the machine enter true Sleep (S3) without spending the time to write hiberfil.sys on every sleep transition — useful on desktops with reliable mains power. Laptops typically benefit from leaving it on so an unexpected battery loss does not cost an unsaved session.

Entries.Name: Home Groups
Entries.Detail: Home Groups was a Windows feature for sharing files between home PCs. It was removed in Windows 10 1803 but residual services may still be present.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: HomeGroup services are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Home Groups was removed in Windows 10 1803, but residual services may still be registered. This cleans up those leftover components.

Entries.Name: Internet Connection Sharing
Entries.Detail: Allows this PC to share its internet connection with other devices, acting as a software hotspot. Enabling shows this option in network settings.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Internet Connection Sharing is prevented
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows this PC to act as a NAT gateway, sharing its internet connection with other devices. Exposes a DHCP server and routing on the sharing adapter.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Internet Connection Sharing is not available on Windows Server.

Entries.Name: Latest .NET Runtime for All Apps
Entries.Description: Use latest installed .NET runtime for all apps
Entries.Detail: Forces 32-bit and 64-bit applications to use the newest installed .NET runtime version instead of the version they were originally built against.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Latest installed .NET runtime not used for all apps
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Forces 32-bit and 64-bit processes to bind to the newest installed .NET runtime rather than the version they were compiled against. Can expose compatibility issues in older applications.

Entries.Name: LLMNR Protocol
Entries.Detail: Link-Local Multicast Name Resolution resolves hostnames on local networks. It can be exploited in credential capture attacks and is best disabled.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: LLMNR protocol is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: LLMNR resolves hostnames on the local network without DNS. It responds to broadcast queries, making it exploitable for credential capture via Responder or similar tools. Disable on any network where you do not explicitly need it.

Entries.Name: NCSI Probe
Entries.Detail: Network Connectivity Status Indicator checks connectivity by pinging Microsoft servers. Disabling may show incorrect network status icons.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Network Connectivity Status Indicator probe is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The Network Connectivity Status Indicator probes Microsoft servers to determine internet reachability and set the network icon. Disabling may cause the icon to show no internet even when connectivity exists.

Entries.Name: NetBIOS over TCP/IP
Entries.Detail: NetBIOS is an older name resolution protocol used for Windows file sharing. Disabling on modern networks reduces unnecessary broadcast traffic.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: NetBIOS over TCP/IP is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: NetBIOS name resolution is exploitable for credential capture on local networks (NBNS poisoning). Modern networks that use DNS exclusively do not need it.

Entries.Name: Network Adapters Save Power
Entries.Description: Allow network adapters to save power
Entries.Detail: Allows Windows to put network adapters into a low-power state when idle. Disabling prevents brief reconnection delays after the adapter wakes up.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Network adapters allowed to save power
Entries.WhyThisMatters: When enabled, Windows can cut adapter power after an idle period. Disabling this prevents brief connection drops that occur when the adapter wakes.

Entries.Name: Network Devices Auto Install
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows automatically downloads and installs drivers for newly connected network devices.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Automatic installation of network devices is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether Windows automatically fetches and installs drivers for newly plugged-in network devices. Disabling requires manual driver installation.

Entries.Name: Network Discovery
Entries.Detail: Enables this PC to find other devices on the local network and be visible to them. Required for browsing network shares in File Explorer.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Network Discovery and File/Printer Sharing are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows this PC to see other devices on the LAN and be seen by them. Required for browsing network shares by name in File Explorer.

Entries.Name: New App Prompt
Entries.Description: 'How do you want to open this file?' prompt
Entries.Detail: When you try to open an unassociated file type, Windows shows a dialog asking how to open it. Disabling suppresses this prompt.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Prompt shown for unassociated file types
Entries.WhyThisMatters: When a file with no associated application is opened, Windows shows a dialog offering to search the Store or browse locally. Disabling suppresses this prompt.

Entries.Name: NTP Server Override
Entries.Detail: Overrides Windows Time to use pool.ntp.org instead of the default Windows time server.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows Time uses the default time server
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Points the Windows Time service at pool.ntp.org instead of the Microsoft time server. Uses the NTP pool's anycast infrastructure for time synchronization.

Entries.Name: Num Lock at Startup
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the Num Lock key is automatically turned on when Windows starts. Enable to always start with the number pad active.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Num Lock disabled at startup
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Forces Num Lock on or off at each startup regardless of the BIOS setting or previous state.

Entries.Name: OpenSSH Server
Entries.Detail: Installs the OpenSSH Server capability, starts the sshd and ssh-agent services, opens the firewall for port 22, creates the authorized_keys file, and normalizes sshd_config for standard user key handling.
Entries.CautionReason: May expose the machine to remote access. Installing OpenSSH Server opens port 22 and enables SSH login.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: OpenSSH Server is not installed
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Installs the OpenSSH Server Windows capability, starts sshd, and opens port 22 in the firewall. After enabling, the machine accepts inbound SSH connections.

Entries.Name: Power Plan
Entries.Detail: Sets the Windows power plan. Balanced scales performance based on load. High Performance keeps the CPU at full speed. Ultimate Performance removes all power-saving throttling for maximum throughput. Custom Power Plan duplicates Ultimate Performance under a stable GUID (57696e68-616e-6365-506f-776572000000) so the plan is recognisable across runs. Ultimate Performance may not be available on all systems.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Power plan set to Balanced
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Balanced scales CPU frequency with demand. High Performance keeps the CPU at maximum clock at the cost of higher power draw. Ultimate Performance removes all remaining throttling and is not available on battery-powered systems by default.

Entries.Name: Processor Minimum State
Entries.Detail: Sets the minimum processor state percentage for AC and DC power.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Processor minimum state is 5% on AC and DC
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the floor for CPU frequency scaling on AC and DC power. Raising it prevents the CPU from dropping to idle clocks but increases power draw at low load.

Entries.Name: Processor Maximum State
Entries.Detail: Sets the maximum processor state percentage for AC and DC power.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Processor maximum state is 100% on AC and DC
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the ceiling for CPU frequency scaling. Lowering below 100% caps thermal and power output at the cost of peak performance.

Entries.Name: Processor Performance Increase Threshold
Entries.Detail: Sets the processor utilization threshold required before increasing performance state.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Processor performance increase threshold is 10%
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The CPU utilization level that must be sustained before Windows increases clock speed. Lower values make the CPU ramp up more eagerly.

Entries.Name: Processor Performance Decrease Threshold
Entries.Detail: Sets the processor utilization threshold required before decreasing performance state.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Processor performance decrease threshold is 8%
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The CPU utilization level that must fall below before Windows reduces clock speed. Higher values keep clocks elevated longer before stepping down.

Entries.Name: Processor Performance Boost Mode
Entries.Detail: Controls how Windows selects a boosted performance state when the processor has headroom.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Processor performance boost mode is Enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Selects how Windows uses processor boost headroom — aggressive, efficient, or autonomous. Affects how quickly and how often the CPU enters boost states.

Entries.Name: CPU Core Parking Minimum Cores
Entries.Detail: Set the minimum percentage of CPU cores that must remain active and responsive.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: CPU core parking minimum cores are 100% on AC and 10% on DC
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the minimum percentage of cores Windows must keep unparked. Lower values allow more aggressive core parking at idle.

Entries.Name: CPU Core Parking Maximum Cores
Entries.Detail: Set the maximum percentage of CPU cores allowed to be active (100% for best performance).
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: CPU core parking maximum cores are 100% on AC and DC
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the maximum percentage of cores Windows is allowed to keep active. Set to 100% to prevent Windows from parking any cores.

Entries.Name: Processor Energy Performance Preference
Entries.Detail: Sets the processor energy performance preference percentage for AC and DC power.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Processor energy performance preference is 25% on AC and 50% on DC
Entries.WhyThisMatters: On modern CPUs with efficiency cores, this hint shifts scheduling toward performance cores (low value) or efficiency cores (high value).

Entries.Name: USB Hub Selective Suspend Timeout
Entries.Detail: Set how long USB hubs wait idle before powering down to save energy.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: USB hub selective suspend timeout is 50 milliseconds on AC and DC
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the idle timeout before USB hubs power down. Shorter values save more power at the cost of a brief delay when the hub wakes.

Entries.Name: USB selective suspend setting
Entries.Detail: Allow Windows to power down individual USB ports when devices are idle to save energy.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: USB selective suspend setting is Disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows Windows to cut power to idle USB ports. Disable if you have a device that loses state or connectivity when the port suspends.

Entries.Name: Intel(R) Graphics Power Plan
Entries.Detail: Balance Intel integrated graphics performance with power consumption and battery life.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Intel(R) Graphics Power Plan is Balanced
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Selects the Intel integrated GPU power profile between maximum battery savings and maximum performance. Only applies to Intel iGPU systems.

Entries.Name: Video Playback Quality Bias
Entries.Detail: Prioritize smooth video playback over battery life when watching videos.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Video playback quality bias is Video playback power-saving bias
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Tells Windows to prioritize smooth video decoding over battery conservation during video playback.

Entries.Name: Power Throttling
Entries.Detail: Controls the PowerThrottlingOff registry flag used by Windows power throttling.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Power throttling is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Power throttling reduces background app CPU frequency to save energy. Disabling it prevents Windows from down-clocking background workloads.

Entries.Name: Prevent Edge Shortcut Creation
Entries.Description: Prevent Edge desktop shortcut on update
Entries.Detail: Stops Microsoft Edge from recreating its desktop shortcut after updates. Applies to all Edge release channels.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Edge desktop shortcut creation is not prevented
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Blocks Edge from re-adding its desktop shortcut after updates and channel upgrades. Applies to Stable, Beta, Dev, and Canary channels.

Entries.Name: QoS Packet Scheduler
Entries.Detail: The Quality of Service packet scheduler can prioritize certain types of network traffic. Required for some network QoS features to work correctly.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: QoS packet scheduler is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The QoS packet scheduler is required for applications that use network traffic prioritization APIs. It does not throttle bandwidth on its own.

Entries.Name: Recommended Troubleshooting
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows automatically runs troubleshooters when problems are detected. Default still asks for your permission before running.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Troubleshooter asks before running (Default)
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether Windows automatically runs built-in troubleshooters when it detects a known problem. Default still asks for permission; disable to prevent automatic troubleshooter runs entirely.

Entries.Name: Registry Backup
Entries.Detail: Enables automatic periodic backup of the Windows registry hive files to the RegBack folder inside the Windows directory and registers the daily AutoRegBackup scheduled task that invokes RegIdleBackup.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Registry backup to RegBack folder and the AutoRegBackup task are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Configures Windows to write periodic backups of the registry hive files to the RegBack folder. Required since Windows 10 1803 disabled this by default.

Entries.Name: Reserved Storage
Entries.Detail: Windows reserves several gigabytes of disk space for updates, temporary files, and caches. Disabling reclaims this reserved space after the next update.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Reserved storage is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Windows reserves a fixed disk allocation for updates, temp files, and OS caches. Disabling reclaims that space after the next feature update applies the change.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Reserved storage is not available on Windows Server.

Entries.Name: Restart After Update
Entries.Description: Restart as soon as possible to finish updating
Entries.Detail: Allows Windows to schedule a restart as soon as possible after completing an update installation.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Device does not restart as soon as possible after update
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows Windows to schedule an immediate restart once an update finishes installing rather than waiting for the next scheduled window.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Automatic restart-after-update behavior is a Windows client update setting.

Entries.Name: Restart Required Notification
Entries.Description: Notify when restart is required for updates
Entries.Detail: Shows a notification in the taskbar when a Windows Update has been installed and requires a restart to complete.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Restart required notification is shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows a persistent taskbar indicator when a pending update is waiting for a restart to complete installation.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Restart-required notifications are a Windows client update setting.

Entries.Name: Update Notification Level
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows shows all update notifications, only restart warnings, or no update notifications.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows Update uses its default notification behavior
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls which Windows Update notifications are shown. Hiding them prevents restart prompts from appearing, which can lead to missed updates if not monitored elsewhere.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Windows Update notification levels are a Windows client experience setting.

Entries.Name: Save Restartable Apps
Entries.Description: Auto save and restart apps on sign-in
Entries.Detail: Windows can save the state of open apps when you sign out and reopen them automatically after your next sign in.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Automatically saving restartable apps is disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Windows saves the state of open apps at sign-out and reopens them automatically at the next sign-in.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Restartable-apps sign-in behavior is a Windows client feature.

Entries.Name: Search App in Store for Unknown Ext.
Entries.Description: Search for app in store for unknown extensions
Entries.Detail: When you open a file with an unknown extension, Windows can suggest looking in the Microsoft Store. Disabling skips this suggestion.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Store suggestion shown for unknown file extensions
Entries.WhyThisMatters: When opening a file with an unrecognized extension, Windows can suggest searching the Store for a compatible app. Disabling skips this Store lookup.

Entries.Name: Block Microsoft Store Search Results
Entries.Detail: Blocks recommended Microsoft Store apps when searching for apps in the Start menu.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Recommended Microsoft Store apps are shown in Start menu search
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Suppresses Store app recommendations from appearing in Start menu app search results alongside locally installed apps.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Microsoft Store search results are not available on Windows Server.

Entries.Name: SMB 1.0 Protocol
Entries.Detail: SMBv1 is an outdated and vulnerable file sharing protocol known for EternalBlue exploits. Disable unless required for very old network devices.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: SMB 1.0 protocol is disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: SMBv1 has no encryption and is the protocol exploited by EternalBlue. It should be disabled unless an old NAS, printer, or legacy device that cannot be updated still requires it.

Entries.Name: Sticky Keys (5x Shift)
Entries.Description: Press Shift 5 times for Sticky Keys
Entries.Detail: Pressing Shift five times quickly triggers the Sticky Keys accessibility dialog. Disable to prevent this from interrupting your work.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Sticky Keys triggered by pressing Shift 5 times
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Pressing Shift five times in rapid succession normally triggers the Sticky Keys accessibility dialog. Disable this if accidental activation interrupts your workflow.

Entries.Name: Storage Sense
Entries.Detail: Automatically frees up disk space on a schedule by deleting temporary files, old downloads, and Recycle Bin contents.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Storage Sense is disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Automatically removes temporary files, empties the Recycle Bin, and clears old Downloads folder items on a configured schedule.

Entries.Name: Unknown Networks Profile
Entries.Detail: Sets the default network profile applied to new or unrecognized network connections when they are first detected.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Unidentified networks set to Public profile
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the network profile assigned when Windows encounters an unrecognized connection. Public restricts discovery and sharing on unknown networks.

Entries.Name: Updates for Other MS Products
Entries.Description: Receive updates for other Microsoft products
Entries.Detail: When enabled, Windows Update also checks for and installs updates for other installed Microsoft software alongside Windows updates.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Updates for other Microsoft products are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Extends Windows Update to service other Microsoft software. Equivalent to enabling the "Receive updates for other Microsoft products" option in Update settings.

Entries.Name: Windows Update Repair
Entries.Detail: Resets Windows Update services, cache, policy, BITS jobs, and re-registers core update DLLs. Aggressive mode also runs CHKDSK, SFC, DISM, and deeper cache resets.
Entries.CautionReason: Repairs Windows Update by stopping services, clearing caches, resetting policies, and re-registering system components. Aggressive mode also runs disk and operating-system repair commands.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Standard repair path
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Resets the Windows Update stack: stops services, clears the download cache, re-registers DLLs, and resets BITS jobs. Aggressive mode adds DISM health checks and SFC scans.

Entries.Name: Microsoft Store App Auto-Download
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Microsoft Store automatically downloads and installs app updates. Disabling keeps Store app updates from downloading in the background.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Microsoft Store app updates are downloaded and installed automatically
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether the Store automatically downloads and installs app updates in the background. Disabling pauses Store updates until you check manually.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Microsoft Store app updates are not available on Windows Server.

Entries.Name: Verbose Startup/Shutdown Messages
Entries.Detail: Shows detailed status messages during Windows startup and shutdown instead of just the generic loading spinner.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Detailed startup/shutdown messages are hidden
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Replaces the generic boot animation with descriptive status messages showing which services and scripts are starting or stopping.

Entries.Name: Win32 Long Path Limit
Entries.Detail: Removes the 260-character maximum file path limit for Win32 applications. Required for some development tools and complex folder structures.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: 260 character path limit is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Removes the 260-character file path limit for Win32 applications by setting LongPathsEnabled in the system registry. Also requires app manifests to opt in.

Entries.Name: Windows Capabilities
Entries.Description: Install or remove optional Windows capabilities via dialog
Entries.Detail: Opens the optional features dialog to add or remove Windows capabilities such as language packs or RSAT tools. This is a package-style change rather than a simple toggle, and some installs can require downloads, source files, or restart follow-up.
Entries.CautionReason: May require Store or winget to recover. Installing or removing Windows capabilities changes optional component packages. Recovery can require Windows Update access, source media, DISM, or a restart instead of a simple toggle reversal.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Optional features managed through dialog
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Opens the optional features dialog for adding or removing Windows capabilities such as language packs, RSAT tools, and handwriting recognition. Some capabilities require internet access or a restart to complete.

Entries.Name: Windows Features
Entries.Detail: Opens the Turn Windows features on or off dialog to enable or disable optional Windows components like Hyper-V or Telnet.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows features managed through dialog
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Opens the Windows Features dialog for enabling or disabling built-in OS components like Hyper-V, Telnet, or TFTP. Changes take effect after a restart.

Entries.Name: Legacy Media
Entries.Description: Enable or disable the legacy media feature bundle
Entries.Detail: Installs or removes Windows Media Player, Media Features, DirectPlay, and Legacy Components as one grouped bundle.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Legacy media features are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Installs or removes the Windows Media Player, Media Features, DirectPlay, and Legacy Components feature bundle. Needed for older games and applications that depend on these components.

PlatformSupport.UnavailableReason: Legacy media features are not available on Windows Server.

Entries.Name: NFS
Entries.Description: Enable or disable the NFS feature bundle
Entries.Detail: Installs or removes the Client for NFS feature bundle and applies the client defaults after enabling.
Entries.CautionReason: Enables Windows NFS client components and applies the default NFS client registry configuration.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: NFS features are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Installs NFS client support and writes the client configuration defaults. Enables mounting remote NFS exports as drive letters.

Entries.Name: Hyper-V Management Tools
Entries.Description: Enable or disable Hyper-V management tools
Entries.Detail: Installs or removes the Hyper-V management tools without enabling the Hyper-V hypervisor.
Entries.CautionReason: Enables Hyper-V management tooling and can affect virtualization workflows.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Hyper-V management tools are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Installs the Hyper-V Manager console and PowerShell cmdlets without enabling the hypervisor itself. Lets you manage remote Hyper-V hosts without hosting VMs locally.

Entries.Name: Windows Manage Default Printer
Entries.Detail: Lets Windows automatically set your most recently used printer as the default. Disabling keeps your manually chosen default printer fixed.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows manages the default printer
Entries.WhyThisMatters: When enabled, Windows automatically promotes the most recently used printer to default. Disabling this keeps your manually selected default printer fixed.
